title: Test Catalyst shield enforcement on macOS
description:
## Why
We need to empirically verify whether ManagedSettingsStore writes from a Mac Catalyst build of ScreenScheduler actually block apps on macOS, and if so, which apps (Catalyst-only vs native macOS).

## Prerequisites
- VM running in GUI/windowed mode (not headless SSH)
- Sign into Xcode on the VM with the net.emclain Apple ID (paid developer account)
- Confirm net.emclain.ScreenScheduler App ID exists in developer portal with Family Controls capability enabled
- Confirm App Group group.net.emclain.ScreenScheduler is registered in portal
- Build and run the ScreenTimeScheduler iOS scheme with destination 'My Mac (Mac Catalyst)'

## Test steps
1. Launch the Catalyst app on the VM Mac
2. Request FamilyControls authorization (.individual) — confirm it succeeds
3. Open FamilyActivityPicker and select one or two apps (pick a mix: one Catalyst app like Feedback Assistant, one native macOS app like Finder or Safari)
4. Write a shield to ManagedSettingsStore for the selected tokens
5. Observe: does the shield UI appear over the Catalyst app? Does the native macOS app get blocked or is it unaffected?
6. Clear the shield and confirm both apps become accessible again

## Expected outcomes to record
- Does FamilyControls authorization succeed on macOS via Catalyst? (yes/no)
- Does FamilyActivityPicker show macOS apps at all?
- Are Catalyst apps blocked? Native macOS apps?
- Any runtime errors (FamilyControlsAgent sandbox errors, etc.)?

Update screen-b5g with findings.

title: Switch to Xcode 16 and verify all targets build
description:
After downloading Xcode 16, run xcode-select to point at it, then build all targets (ScreenTimeScheduler iOS, ScreenTimeSchedulerMac, and the 3 extension targets). Xcode 16 ships with the macOS 13-15 SDK where FamilyControls, ManagedSettings, and DeviceActivity are available on native macOS — unlike Xcode 26 which dropped them.

What to do:
1. sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode16.app/Contents/Developer (adjust path)
2. xcodebuild -scheme ScreenTimeScheduler -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
3. xcodebuild -scheme ScreenTimeSchedulerMac -destination 'platform=macOS' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
4. Build extension targets

Then audit the #if os(iOS) guards added in commit 2b33aeb — if DeviceActivityName, DeviceActivityCenter, and FamilyActivitySelection are available on macOS in Xcode 16, remove those guards so the macOS app retains its full capability (ScheduleManager.register(), TokenStore.selection/save, CapabilityProbe).
close_reason: Xcode 16 builds clean: iOS ✓, macOS ✓ (shield section removed, iOS-only APIs unavailable on native macOS), Catalyst ✓ (SUPPORTS_MACCATALYST enabled). Bundle ID renamed to net.emclain.ScreenScheduler throughout. iOS guards from 2b33aeb confirmed correct.

title: Fix pbxproj: path doubling and macOS ExtensionKit product type
notes:
Two issues prevent building:

1. **Path doubling in all group/file references**: Group paths use full paths from root (e.g. 'ScreenTimeScheduler/App/iOS') instead of relative folder names (e.g. 'iOS'). Since sourceTree='<group>' means relative-to-parent, Xcode compounds them, producing paths like:
   ScreenTimeScheduler/ScreenTimeScheduler/App/ScreenTimeScheduler/App/iOS/App/iOS/ScreenTimeSchedulerApp.swift
   
   Fix: Each group path should be just its own folder name. Each file path should be just the filename. Affects ALL groups and file references in the pbxproj.

2. **macOS extension targets use wrong product type**: The 3 macOS extension targets (DeviceActivityMonitorExtensionMac, ShieldConfigurationExtensionMac, ShieldActionExtensionMac) use 'com.apple.product-type.extensionkit.extension' which fails with 'unable to resolve product type for platform macOS' on Xcode 26.4. Need to determine the correct product type - likely 'com.apple.product-type.app-extension'.

Both require editing ScreenTimeScheduler.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj directly. Cannot be done from within Xcode (crashes risk). Use CLI agent session with Xcode closed.

The source code itself is fine - all Swift files compile-ready. Only the project file needs fixing.
close_reason: Fixed path doubling (14 groups, 33 file refs) and changed extensionkit.extension to app-extension for 3 extension targets

title: Hardware-run CapabilityProbe on iMac 2017: record atomicPerTokenUnshield result
description:
The probe infrastructure now compiles (ShieldCapability defined, screen-72b). The empirical result for atomicPerTokenUnshield on macOS 13 is still unknown — it requires running CapabilityProbe.runAll() on the actual iMac 2017 hardware per TEST_SETUP.md.

**What needs to happen:**
1. Follow TEST_SETUP.md to prepare the iMac 2017 (macOS 13 Ventura, Apple ID, Family Sharing).
2. Build and run the macOS target; tap 'Run Probes'.
3. Capture the ProbeReport output (in-app display + Console.app).
4. Update CapabilityMatrix.macOS13Support(): replace .atomicPerTokenUnshield: .unknown with .enforced or .silentNoOp based on result.
5. If .silentNoOp, file a bead to widen OverrideEngine grants from app-scoped to group-wide on macOS 13.

This is the manual follow-through step that screen-72b (code) unblocks.
notes: Code is complete (CapabilityProbe.runAll() wired, CapabilityMatrix.macOS13Support() has .atomicPerTokenUnshield: .unknown at line 176). This issue is purely a hardware gate: run the macOS target on iMac 2017, tap 'Run Probes', read the ProbeReport, and flip .unknown to .enforced or .silentNoOp. No code changes possible without hardware results.

title: Write iMac hardware test prerequisites document
description:
No document exists telling a human what they need to install and configure on the iMac 2017 before running the hardware verification tests. Without this, screen-72b, screen-8ky, screen-n4v, and screen-uc3 cannot be executed.

**What needs to be documented (create TEST_SETUP.md in repo root):**

### Required hardware / OS
- iMac 2017, macOS 13 Ventura (not 14+) — the specific platform under test

### On the Mac doing the build (may differ from iMac)
- Xcode 15 or later (minimum that supports macOS 13 deployment target reliably)
- Apple Developer Program membership ($99/yr) — required for the development variant of com.apple.developer.family-controls entitlement (auto-granted, no review)
- In Xcode → Preferences → Accounts: add the Apple ID that holds the Developer Program membership
- In Xcode signing settings: set Team to that account; entitlement is in ScreenTimeSchedulerMac.entitlements

### On the iMac under test
- macOS 13 Ventura installed
- Signed in to iCloud with the child's Apple ID (for familyControlsChildAuth test) OR parent's Apple ID (for atomicPerTokenUnshield test — .individual auth suffices)
- Family Sharing configured: parent Apple ID must have the child Apple ID in their Family group, with Screen Time passcode set
- Parent device (iPhone or Mac) must be nearby and unlocked to approve the .child authorization request
- Console.app open, filtered to subsystem 'com.example.screen-time-scheduler' to capture OSLog output from CapabilityProbe

### Running the tests
1. Build macOS target from Xcode on the build Mac (or the iMac itself if it has Xcode)
2. Copy .app to iMac if building remotely (or use Xcode direct device run)
3. Run probe UI bead's 'Run Probes' button → capture output from Console.app or in-app display
4. Run FamilyActivityPicker UI bead's manual test for per-token shield verification
5. Sleep/wake test: put iMac to sleep (Apple menu → Sleep), wait 60s, wake, watch Console for DAM callback within 2 min
6. Paste findings into CapabilityMatrix.swift, replacing .unknown entries with .enforced / .silentNoOp / .crashes

**One-line execution plan:** Read TEST_SETUP.md → install prereqs → run probe app → follow test steps → update CapabilityMatrix.swift.
acceptance_criteria: A human with no prior context can read TEST_SETUP.md and execute all hardware verification tests without asking any follow-up questions

title: Add FamilyActivityPicker + shield test UI to macOS ContentView
description:
screen-uc3 requires manually picking 3 apps via FamilyActivityPicker, shielding all 3, removing one token, and verifying that only that one app becomes accessible. The iOS ContentView has FamilyActivityPicker wired up; the macOS ContentView is a stub.

**What needs to be done:**
Add to ScreenTimeScheduler/App/macOS/ContentView.swift:
1. FamilyControls authorization request button (calls AuthorizationCenter.shared.requestAuthorization(for: .individual))
2. FamilyActivityPicker sheet to select apps (store tokens in TokenStore)
3. 'Shield All' button — writes all selected tokens to ManagedSettingsStore.shield.applications
4. Per-token 'Unshield' buttons — removes one token from the active set, leaves others
5. 'Clear All' button — sets shield.applications = nil
6. Current shield state display (list which tokens are currently shielded)

**One-line execution plan:** Build macOS target on iMac 2017 → authorize → pick 3 apps → Shield All → Unshield one → alt-tab to verify that one app opens while others are blocked → re-shield → update CapabilityMatrix.atomicPerTokenUnshield.

**Why separate from the probe UI bead:**
This requires real ApplicationTokens from FamilyActivityPicker and human observation; the probe bead is automated runtime checks. They may be implemented in the same ContentView but are separate implementation tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Can pick apps, shield all, unshield one at a time, and observe which apps are blocked on macOS 13

title: Add Run Probes button to macOS ContentView
description:
The macOS app ContentView is a stub (Text placeholder). CapabilityProbe.runAll() exists in Core/Scheduling/CapabilityProbe.swift but is never called.

**What needs to be done:**
Add a 'Run Probes' button to ScreenTimeScheduler/App/macOS/ContentView.swift that:
1. Calls CapabilityProbe(log: logger).runAll() asynchronously
2. Displays the ProbeReport.summary string in a ScrollView/TextEditor in the same window
3. Shows a spinner while running
4. Includes a 'Copy to Clipboard' button so results can be pasted into a bug report

**One-line execution plan:** Build macOS target on iMac 2017 → tap 'Run Probes' → copy output → paste into CapabilityMatrix.swift comments and update .unknown entries.

**Why this blocks hardware verification:**
screen-72b (atomicPerTokenUnshield), screen-8ky (familyControlsChildAuth), and screen-n4v (all .unknown entries) all require running CapabilityProbe.runAll() on the target hardware. Without a UI entrypoint that code is dead.
acceptance_criteria: macOS app builds and runs on macOS 13; tapping Run Probes produces a ProbeReport.summary string visible in the app window
close_reason: Added Run Probes button to macOS ContentView: async probe run, ScrollView output, Copy to Clipboard

title: agent-start.sh: push claim commit before starting work
description: agent-start.sh claims an issue in the local Dolt DB then starts work, giving a multi-minute race window where a second agent can claim the same issue. Fix: push the claim commit immediately after creating it and abort if the push is rejected. This shrinks the race window from the full work duration to a single push round-trip.

title: Merge driver: detect and reject double-claim on same issue
description: The issues.jsonl merge driver silently picks a winner when two agents both claim the same issue. It should detect the double-claim signature (both sides transition status open→in_progress relative to ancestor, with different assignees) and exit non-zero so git falls back to conflict markers. This makes the race visible at landing time rather than silently dropping one agent's claim.

title: Verify atomicPerTokenUnshield on iMac 2017 hardware (PLAN.md risk 11)
description: CapabilityMatrix.macOS13Support() marks atomicPerTokenUnshield as .unknown. Must be verified on the iMac 2017 target before building OverrideEngine. Run CapabilityProbe.runAll() on the device during first-week spike. If absent, OverrideEngine must widen app-scoped grants to group-wide on macOS 13.

title: Verify familyControlsChildAuth (.child) on iMac 2017 hardware (PLAN.md risk 9)
description: CapabilityMatrix.macOS13Support() marks familyControlsChildAuth as .unknown. Must be verified on the iMac 2017 with the child Apple ID before building onboarding. Run CapabilityProbe.runAll() during onboarding spike. If .child fails, fall back to .individual and update CapabilityMatrix + document the weakened tamper-resistance guarantee.

title: Test DAM callback reliability on macOS 13 Ventura
description:
**Addresses Open Risk #3.**

On the iMac 2017 running macOS 13 Ventura, test DeviceActivityMonitor reliability:

1. Register a DAM schedule with a short interval (e.g., 5 minutes)
2. Let the Mac run through multiple intervalDidStart/intervalDidEnd cycles
3. Document: do all callbacks fire? Any missed?
4. Put the Mac to sleep, wake it during a scheduled interval
5. Document: does the callback fire on wake? Delayed? Missed entirely?
6. Test across overnight sleep (close lid, open in morning)

Results inform whether the LaunchAgent wake-nudge mitigation is necessary and sufficient.

Update PLAN.md Open Risk #3 with findings.

title: Test FC .child authorization on macOS 13 Ventura
description:
**Critical unknown from Open Risk #9.**

On the iMac 2017 running macOS 13 Ventura, signed in as a Family Sharing child Apple ID:

1. Attempt to request FamilyControls .child authorization
2. Document exactly what happens:
   - Does the API call succeed?
   - Does it prompt for guardian approval?
   - Does it fail with an error? What error?
   - Does it silently return .notDetermined or .denied?
3. If .child fails, test .individual as fallback
4. Document the UX implications of each outcome

Results determine whether the child iMac can have tamper-resistant enforcement or only soft-lock (.individual) enforcement.

Update PLAN.md Open Risk #9 with findings.

title: Implement ShieldConfiguration and ShieldAction extensions
description:
Implement the shield UI extensions:

**ShieldConfigurationExtension:**
- Return a basic ShieldConfiguration with a message like 'This app is blocked by your schedule'
- Customize appearance minimally (title, subtitle, icon)

**ShieldActionExtension:**
- Expose a single 'Close' action (no AFMT yet)
- Log when the extension is invoked
- Return .close to dismiss the shield

These extensions prove the shield UI pipeline works. AFMT comes in a later milestone.

title: Implement shield apply/clear via ManagedSettingsStore
description:
Wire up the DAM callbacks to apply and clear shields:

- In intervalDidStart: read tokens from App Group, write to ManagedSettingsStore.shield.applications
- In intervalDidEnd: clear ManagedSettingsStore.shield.applications
- Handle errors gracefully and log them
- Test that picked apps are actually blocked when shield is applied
- Test that apps are accessible when shield is cleared

This is the core enforcement mechanism. Must work before anything else matters.

title: Implement hardcoded schedule and DAM registration
description:
Create a hardcoded single-window schedule and register it with DeviceActivityMonitor:

- Define a Window struct with weekdays, start time, end time
- Hardcode one window (e.g., block picked apps 9:00-17:00 on weekdays)
- Register a DeviceActivitySchedule with DAM for this window
- DAM extension implements intervalDidStart and intervalDidEnd callbacks
- Log all DAM callbacks to OSLog with timestamps

No dynamic schedule changes - the window is compile-time constant for this milestone.

title: Implement FamilyActivityPicker to capture tokens
description:
Add a minimal UI that presents FamilyActivityPicker and stores the selected tokens:

- Request FamilyControls authorization (.individual for testing, .child later)
- Present FamilyActivityPicker in the main app
- Store selected ApplicationToken set in App Group UserDefaults or a simple file
- Log the token count and hashes (not raw bytes) to OSLog

This is the prerequisite for having tokens to shield. No schedule logic yet - just pick and persist.

title: Create Xcode project skeleton with all targets
description:
Create the bare-bones Xcode project structure:

- Main app target (iOS/iPadOS)
- Main app target (macOS)
- DeviceActivityMonitorExtension (iOS + macOS)
- ShieldConfigurationExtension (iOS + macOS)
- ShieldActionExtension (iOS + macOS)
- App Group container (group.com.example.sts)
- Shared Swift code directory (Core/)

Configure development entitlements:
- com.apple.developer.family-controls (development)
- com.apple.developer.deviceactivity
- App Group
- Background modes: remote-notification (for later)

No UI beyond a basic 'Hello World' view. Goal is a project that builds and runs on both platforms.
close_reason: Xcode project skeleton completed in commit 2b50e1a

title: Milestone 1: Shield One App on One Device
description:
Prove the core enforcement loop works end-to-end on a single platform (iPadOS or macOS), reducing the highest-priority technical risks before building out the full sync/multi-device architecture.

**What it validates:**
- macOS 13 Ventura API parity (Open Risk #2)
- macOS DAM reliability (Open Risk #3)
- FamilyControls .child availability on macOS 13 (Open Risk #9)
- macOS 13 per-token unshield (Open Risk #11)
- Token portability UX (Open Risk #4)

**Deliverables:**
1. Bare-bones Xcode project with main app (iOS + macOS), DAM extension, ShieldConfiguration extension, ShieldAction extension, App Group, development entitlements
2. Hardcoded single-window schedule (no CloudKit, no editor) - one Window blocking a picked app during a time range
3. DAM registration that fires intervalDidStart/intervalDidEnd to apply/clear shields
4. ShieldActionExtension that exposes 'Close' (no AFMT yet)
5. Logging to OSLog under com.example.sts
6. Test matrix documenting empirical results on both macOS 13 and iPadOS 26

**Defers:** CloudKit sync, multi-device coordination, AFMT flow, schedule editor UI, macOS daemon, NDJSON logging

title: Document Xcode 16 requirement in AGENTS.md
description:
Add a note to AGENTS.md and/or CLAUDE.md that the macOS target requires Xcode 16 (not Xcode 26+). Xcode 26 drops FamilyControls, ManagedSettings, and DeviceActivity from the native macOS SDK, breaking the macOS build.

Context: Apple's FamilyControls and ManagedSettings APIs are documented as iOS/Mac Catalyst only. They happened to be available in the native macOS SDK up through Xcode 15/16 (macOS 13-15 SDK), but were removed in the Xcode 26 SDK. The project targets macOS 13 with MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET=13.0 and depends on this SDK availability.

Note to add: something like 'macOS target requires Xcode 16 or earlier. Xcode 26+ drops Screen Time APIs (FamilyControls, ManagedSettings, DeviceActivity) from the native macOS SDK. See bead screen-XXX for details.'

title: iOS DAM extension: add missed-callback detection and align logging with macOS
description:
The iOS DeviceActivityMonitorExtension (ScreenTimeScheduler/Extensions/DeviceActivityMonitorExtension/iOS/) is missing the sequence-counter missed-callback detection that the macOS extension has (PLAN.md Open Risk #3). It also uses a different subsystem name ('com.example.sts.DAMExtension' vs 'com.example.sts') and a Unix timestamp (timeIntervalSince1970) instead of ISO 8601, making cross-platform log correlation harder.

What needs to happen:
1. Copy the damStartSeqKey/damEndSeqKey constants and the incrementSeq/currentSeq helpers from the macOS extension.
2. Add missed-callback detection logic in intervalDidStart and intervalDidEnd (same as macOS).
3. Fix the subsystem to 'com.example.sts' (drop the .DAMExtension suffix) so log predicates match both platforms.
4. Replace Date.now.timeIntervalSince1970 with isoNow() so timestamps are readable.

title: merge-issues-jsonl.py: no warning when merge changes a bead's status
description:
When a git merge changes a bead's status from open to closed, the merge driver does so silently. The agent has no way to distinguish a legitimate remote close from a phantom one, and may incorrectly assume work is incomplete (as occurred with screen-27h).

**Observed incident:** screen-27h was created locally as open, then a merge from origin/main brought in a remote version with status=closed and a later updated_at. The merge driver correctly picked the closed version (another agent had done the work), but from the local side it looked like a phantom close with no explanation.

**Root cause:** merge-issues-jsonl.py emits no output when a status transition occurs during merge. The agent only discovers the change by re-querying the bead after the merge.

**Fix:** After writing the merged output, print a warning to stderr for any bead whose status changed during the merge, e.g.:
  warning: screen-27h: status changed open → closed during merge (updated_at: 2026-04-12T18:31:00Z)

This gives the agent a signal to inspect the bead and verify whether the close was legitimate before acting on incorrect assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: A locally-created open bead cannot be silently closed by a merge from a remote that has a stale or phantom closed entry for the same ID
close_reason: Added status-change warnings to merge-issues-jsonl.py; prints to stderr when a merge transitions a bead's status (e.g. open → closed)

title: Hardware verify: macOS 13 Ventura .unknown CapabilityMatrix entries
description: screen-t8a created CapabilityMatrix.swift with empirically-unknown entries for macOS 13: atomicPerTokenUnshield, shieldWebDomains, webContentFilter, damCallbacksReliableAcrossSleepWake. These must be verified by running test code on the iMac 2017 hardware and updating the macOS13Support() entries in CapabilityMatrix.swift. If atomicPerTokenUnshield is absent, OverrideEngine must widen app-scoped grants to group-wide (PLAN.md §11). The .unknown status acts as the TODO marker; this bead tracks the hardware test pass.
notes: Code audit 2026-04-12: shieldWebDomains and webContentFilter are already .silentNoOp in macOS13Support() (documentation-based, no hardware needed). Remaining .unknown entries requiring hardware: atomicPerTokenUnshield and damCallbacksReliableAcrossSleepWake. familyControlsChildAuth is tracked separately in screen-rjz. CapabilityProbe.swift already has runAll() wired up; Run Probes button is in place per screen-r4a. Only actual iMac 2017 hardware run is needed to close this.

title: Document Milestone 1 test results in TEST_RESULTS.md
description:
After running all tests, create TEST_RESULTS.md documenting:

| Test | Platform | Expected | Actual | Notes |
|------|----------|----------|--------|-------|
| FC .child auth | macOS 13 | Unknown | | |
| FC .individual auth | macOS 13 | Works | | |
| FC .child auth | iPadOS 26 | Works | | |
| DAM intervalDidStart | macOS 13 | Works | | |
| DAM intervalDidEnd | macOS 13 | Works | | |
| DAM after sleep/wake | macOS 13 | Unknown | | |
| Shield applies | macOS 13 | Works | | |
| Shield clears | macOS 13 | Works | | |
| Per-token subtract | macOS 13 | Unknown | | |
| ShieldAction fires | macOS 13 | Unknown | | |
| All above | iPadOS 26 | Works | | |

This becomes the empirical basis for CapabilityMatrix and informs what workarounds are needed.

title: Test per-token shield manipulation on macOS 13
description:
**Addresses Open Risk #11.**

On macOS 13 Ventura, test whether ManagedSettingsStore supports fine-grained token manipulation:

1. Pick 3 apps via FamilyActivityPicker
2. Shield all 3 (write all tokens to shield.applications)
3. Remove one token from the shield set (subtract single token)
4. Verify: is the one app now accessible while others remain blocked?
5. Re-add the token
6. Verify: is the app blocked again?

If this fails, app-scoped AFMT grants on macOS 13 must degrade to group-wide grants.

Update PLAN.md Open Risk #11 with findings. Populate CapabilityMatrix accordingly.
notes: Hardware gate: requires iMac 2017 running macOS 13 Ventura. All code scaffolding is in place (FamilyActivityPicker UI via screen-k3a, shield apply/clear via screen-r3f). When hardware is available on 2026-04-19, run the 6-step test in the issue description and update CapabilityMatrix.atomicPerTokenUnshield (.enforced or .silentNoOp) and PLAN.md Open Risk #11 with findings. If per-token subtraction is broken, OverrideEngine must widen app-scoped grants to group-wide on macOS 13.

title: Set up OSLog logging infrastructure
description:
Create a minimal logging layer using OSLog:

- Subsystem: com.example.sts
- Categories: auth, dam, shield, sync (sync unused this milestone but define it)
- Helper functions: logDebug, logInfo, logWarn, logError
- Log to unified logging so Console.app and 'log show' work
- Include stable event names for key operations (auth_requested, auth_granted, dam_interval_start, dam_interval_end, shield_applied, shield_cleared)

Keep it simple - just OSLog wrappers. NDJSON file logging is a later milestone.
close_reason: Logging.swift added to Core/ with Logger extensions, LogEvent constants, and logDebug/logInfo/logWarn/logError wrappers; wired into Xcode project for iOS and macOS app targets

title: Compare revised PLAN_A and PLAN_B; produce PLAN_AB
description:
PLAN_A and PLAN_B look very similar to the human reader. Analyze the two (after they have been revised by the sibling beads — this task is blocked on those) and write PLAN_AB.md.

Structure of PLAN_AB.md:

1. Preamble answering:
   - What are the actual differences between PLAN_A and PLAN_B?
   - Which is best on which axis?
   - Are they substantively different, or basically the same plan with different emphasis?
   - Does it make sense to pick one, or fold them into a single hybrid?

2. Then either:
   (a) State the choice (A or B) with justification, and use the chosen plan's content as the body of PLAN_AB.md, OR
   (b) Write a hybrid plan that takes the best of both.

After writing, edit PLAN_AB.md for concision: remove duplication (both within the new doc and with what already exists in RESEARCH.md), tighten language, make it easier to scan.

Commit and land PLAN_AB.md per the workflow in AGENTS.md (agent-land.sh).

Dependency note: this task should run after the revise-PLAN_A and revise-PLAN_B beads land, so it compares the revised plans, not the originals.

title: Revise PLAN_B to address critiques, updated RESEARCH, and new user requirements
description:
Revise PLAN_B.md in place. Make it reflect new information and address criticism, then tighten the prose.

Sources to consult (read only the PLAN_B-relevant sections in the critiques):
- RESEARCH.md — updated since PLAN_B was written, primarily with info about developer workflows that make Apple entitlements less onerous. Incorporate this into PLAN_B's entitlement/deployability discussion.
- CRITIQUE_1.md, CRITIQUE_2.md, CRITIQUE_3.md — read ONLY the sections that reference PLAN_B. For each criticism, either defend the decision inline in the relevant section of PLAN_B, or modify the plan to adopt the critic's better idea. Integrate the response briefly into the discussion, don't append a separate rebuttal section.

New requirements from the user (must be reflected in the revised plan):

1. Child devices to support today are both macOS (iMac 2017 on Ventura) and iPadOS 26 (iPad). The plan must cover enforcement on both, not iOS only. Other Macs are involved as parent/management devices — those are newer hardware running Sequoia or Tahoe, so API parity concerns that apply to the iMac do not apply to them. However, the "always-on" Mac that PLAN_B centers around could itself be the Ventura iMac (e.g. if it's left running in the common area); this is an option, not necessarily the case. The plan should note what capabilities or workarounds are needed if the always-on Mac is Ventura vs. Sequoia/Tahoe.

2. The user is skeptical of the hybrid "system Downtime + our app shield" design. Their concern: UI confusion, split enforcement boundaries, and it doesn't actually reduce development work. Reconsider whether the hybrid is worth it, or whether a single enforcement regime is cleaner. Defend or drop accordingly.

3. AFMT requirement is relaxed. You do NOT need to replicate Apple's exact "request more time" UI. The core requirement is:
   - After an app has been shielded, the child can request more time
   - Parent receives a real-time notification on Mac/iOS
   - Parent picks from a few options (reject / +15m / +1h / rest-of-day, where "rest-of-day" means until local midnight)
   - That choice flows back to the child device in real time and unlocks for the granted duration
   This can be an overlapping schedule window, an override, or an orthogonal feature — author's choice.

4. It should be possible for a single device to function both as a management surface and also a target. We've been talking about "adult device" and "child device", but the shielding functionality is valuable for adult devices as well. The plan should model this cleanly — management and enforcement are orthogonal roles a device can hold independently.

5. The child UI session on macOS is running under a non-privileged user account.

After the revision, edit PLAN_B.md for concision: remove duplication, tighten language, make it easier to scan.

Commit and land PLAN_B.md per the workflow in AGENTS.md (agent-land.sh).

title: Revise PLAN_A to address critiques, updated RESEARCH, and new user requirements
description:
Revise PLAN_A.md in place. Make it reflect new information and address criticism, then tighten the prose.

Sources to consult (read only the PLAN_A-relevant sections in the critiques):
- RESEARCH.md — updated since PLAN_A was written, primarily with info about developer workflows that make Apple entitlements less onerous. Incorporate this into PLAN_A's entitlement/deployability discussion.
- CRITIQUE_1.md, CRITIQUE_2.md, CRITIQUE_3.md — read ONLY the sections that reference PLAN_A. For each criticism, either defend the decision inline in the relevant section of PLAN_A, or modify the plan to adopt the critic's better idea. Integrate the response briefly into the discussion, don't append a separate rebuttal section.

New requirements from the user (must be reflected in the revised plan):

1. Child devices to support today are both macOS (iMac 2017 on Ventura) and iPadOS 26 (iPad). The plan must cover enforcement on both, not iOS only.

2. The user is skeptical of the hybrid "system Downtime + our app shield" design. Their concern: UI confusion, split enforcement boundaries, and it doesn't actually reduce development work. Reconsider whether the hybrid is worth it, or whether a single enforcement regime is cleaner. Defend or drop accordingly.

3. AFMT requirement is relaxed. You do NOT need to replicate Apple's exact "request more time" UI. The core requirement is:
   - After an app has been shielded, the child can request more time
   - Parent receives a real-time notification on Mac/iOS
   - Parent picks from a few options (reject / +15m / +1h / rest-of-day, where "rest-of-day" means until local midnight)
   - That choice flows back to the child device in real time and unlocks for the granted duration
   This can be an overlapping schedule window, an override, or an orthogonal feature — author's choice.

After the revision, edit PLAN_A.md for concision: remove duplication, tighten language, make it easier to scan.

Commit and land PLAN_A.md per the workflow in AGENTS.md (agent-land.sh).

title: Track macOS 26 SDK Screen Time API removal as future debt
description:
In the macOS 26 SDK (Xcode 26), Apple eliminated all Screen Time enforcement paths on Mac:

**Native macOS**: FamilyControls, ManagedSettings, and DeviceActivity APIs are all marked @available(macOS, unavailable) — won't compile.

**Mac Catalyst**: The framework exists under iOSSupport/ but key APIs (AuthorizationStatus, FamilyActivitySelection, AuthorizationCenter, etc.) are individually marked @available(macCatalyst, unavailable) — won't compile.

**Designed for iPad on Mac**: Compiles and runs, but FamilyControlsAgent refuses the connection at runtime with Error Domain=NSCocoaErrorDomain Code=4099, error 159 — Sandbox restriction. Tested with an active paid developer account and the entitlement provisioned.

**Conclusion**: There is no third-party Screen Time enforcement path on macOS 26. The prior description's claim that 'Catalyst apps can only shield other Catalyst apps' was incorrect — Catalyst cannot use the enforcement stack at all. Xcode 16 (macOS 13–15 SDK) remains the only viable path for Mac enforcement while Apple's stance is unchanged.

**Tested**: 2026-04-12, macOS 26 beta, Xcode 26, paid developer account active.

Decisions needed:
1. Drop the macOS target and go iOS-only for macOS 26+
2. Keep macOS target but require Xcode 16 / macOS 13-15 SDK (current plan)
3. Explore MDM/configuration profile approach for native Mac enforcement

title: Reduce issues.jsonl merge conflicts from concurrent agents
description:
Two agents writing to issues.jsonl concurrently produce a 3-way merge conflict that requires manual resolution. In this session, agent-start.sh failed because issues.jsonl had local uncommitted changes (fixed in bd-setup.sh), and then a content conflict arose because one agent had closed screen-3f2 while another re-opened it.

Root cause: issues.jsonl is a flat file with the full bead state; any concurrent write produces a conflict that git cannot auto-resolve.

Options to investigate:
1. Use a merge driver (.gitattributes + a custom merge tool) that takes the 'newest updated_at wins per issue-id' strategy
2. Switch to an append-only log (one line = one event) that is trivially auto-mergeable
3. Document a locking convention so only one agent writes at a time

The bd-setup.sh fix (commit dirty issues.jsonl before pull) reduces one class of failure but doesn't prevent content conflicts when two agents genuinely update the same bead.

title: Research CKShare-to-child under-13 Apple ID quirks for RESEARCH §4
description:
RESEARCH.md §4 says nothing about under-13 CKShare behavior, but multiple documents reference it as if it does:

- RESEARCH.md line 116 says 'The under-13 CKShare quirks RESEARCH §4 mentions' — but §4 doesn't mention them
- CRITIQUE_3 line 61 says 'Child Apple ID under 13 has historically had quirky CKShare behavior (acknowledged in B, ignored in A)'
- CRITIQUE_2 line 96 says 'CKShare to child Apple IDs has been unreliable enough that treating it as primary is unwise'
- CRITIQUE_1 line 10 references 'RESEARCH §4 and Plan_A Open Risk #5 both flag CKShare-to-child tightening'

This is a circular reference — everyone cites the claim, nobody substantiates it.

Task: research what the actual CKShare-to-child issues are (Apple Dev Forums, developer blog posts, framework changelogs) and update RESEARCH.md §4 with concrete details:
- What specifically fails or behaves differently with under-13 Apple IDs?
- Which iOS/macOS versions are affected?
- Has Apple fixed any of these in recent releases?
- Does this affect the Development CloudKit environment differently than Production?

This determines whether the QR-bootstrap-first design in PLAN_AB is justified or overengineered.
close_reason: Research complete: No documented CKShare restrictions for under-13 child Apple IDs. Added §4.1 to RESEARCH.md with findings. The circular references in project docs were unfounded claims.

title: Test ManagedSettings capability parity on macOS 13 Ventura
description:
PLAN_AB references a CapabilityMatrix that disables unsupported shield types per device, but never enumerates which ManagedSettings keys actually work on macOS 13 Ventura. This is an empirical question that must be answered on the iMac 2017 hardware during implementation.

Keys to test:
- shieldApplications (per-app blocking via ApplicationToken)
- shieldApplicationCategories (category-level blocking via ActivityCategoryToken)
- webContent (web domain filtering via WebDomainToken)
- Account/passcode lock keys

For each key: does writing it to the ManagedSettingsStore actually enforce on Ventura? Does it silently no-op? Does it crash?

Results populate the CapabilityMatrix entries for platform=macOS, osVersion=13.x.

Secondary: test DeviceActivityMonitor callback reliability across sleep/wake cycles on the same hardware, since PLAN_AB flags this as a known risk with only a LaunchAgent wake-nudge as mitigation.
notes: CapabilityMatrix.swift scaffold created at Core/Scheduling/CapabilityMatrix.swift. Data model defines Capability enum (shieldApplications, shieldApplicationCategories, shieldWebDomains, webContentFilter, accountRestrictions, atomicPerTokenUnshield, damCallbacksReliableAcrossSleepWake) and Support enum (enforced/silentNoOp/crashes/unknown). macOS 13 entries populated from Apple docs: shieldApplications and shieldApplicationCategories marked enforced (documented in macOS 13 SDK); accountRestrictions marked silentNoOp (iOS-only keys in macOS 13 headers); shieldWebDomains/webContentFilter/atomicPerTokenUnshield/damCallbacksReliableAcrossSleepWake remain .unknown pending hardware testing. CapabilityMatrix.current returns correct matrix for the running platform/OS. Hardware tests on iMac 2017 must update the macOS13Support() entries; if atomicPerTokenUnshield is absent, OverrideEngine must widen app-scoped grants to group-wide per PLAN.md §11.

title: Setup script should fix git remote and .beads permissions
description:
Two friction points hit during agent onboarding:

1. **Git remote uses SSH but sandbox has no SSH** - The remote is configured as git@github.com:... but sandboxes don't have SSH. Setup should detect this and reconfigure to HTTPS with $GITHUB_TOKEN if available.

2. **.beads directory permissions** - bd warns if .beads has 0755 instead of 0700. This warning pollutes stdout and can corrupt exports. Setup should chmod 700 .beads after init.

Both are one-liners to add to scripts/setup.sh.
close_reason: Added git remote SSH→HTTPS conversion and .beads chmod 700 to setup.sh

title: macOS Ventura: ShieldActionExtension misfire has no fallback for AFMT requests
description:
PLAN_AB's 60s CloudKit polling mitigates missed push notifications for override *decisions*, but does nothing if the ShieldActionExtension fails to fire in the first place — the request never reaches CloudKit, so there's nothing to poll for.

Possible mitigation: main app offers an in-app 'Request more time' button as a fallback when the extension fails.

However, this may not be a real problem. Apple's built-in AFMT presumably uses the same ShieldActionExtension mechanism, and in practice it seems reliable on macOS. Defer until empirical testing on the iMac 2017 confirms whether extension misfires actually occur for shield-tap actions (as opposed to DAM scheduling callbacks, which are known flaky).

value: After any bd write operation (dep add, update, close, etc.) outside of agent-land.sh, you must manually export and commit: chmod 700 .beads && bd export > .beads/issues.jsonl && git add .beads/issues.jsonl && git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m 'bd sync: ...' && git push
